Form of 

Statement of Work & Retained Services AgreementToMaster Services AgreementDated [MONTH DAY, YEAR] 

Services & Outcomes

Contractor is an experienced direct response copywriter and business growth consultant who has helped clients generate over $10 million in incremental revenue. Contractor uses their understanding of persuasion, copywriting, and consumer behavior to help businesses increase their conversion rates and sales.

Contractor Services may be broadly categorized as:

Copywriting for sales pages, advertorials, emails, print materials, and other direct response mediums

Persuasion and consumer behavior consulting

Sales, conversion funnel, and customer acquisition and retention strategy

Business strategy for upsells, cross-sells, and customer lifetime value maximization

Analytics reporting, testing, and iteration

Project management, execution, and implementation 

Without limiting Section 4 (“Warranties”) in the Master Services Agreement (MSA), Services provided by Contractor will attempt to provide benefits for Client, such as:

Client would increase annual profits by hundreds of thousands or millions of US dollars if even one project delivered a 10% increase in sales — which is not uncommon for past Contractor projects.

Increase customer satisfaction, referrals, and retention, while decreasing returns, churn, and their related costs.

When on retainer, Contractor will gain a more detailed and nuanced understanding of Client’s products, voice, and brand. This knowledge increases over time, and will allow for greater consistency in Client’s copy and messaging.

Deliver higher quality work with faster timelines (compared to Client having to hire and invest time to onboard new writers frequently).

Prevent Client projects from being delayed because of Contractor’s availability. Under a retainer, Client will get ongoing, guaranteed access to Contractor’s expertise and project calendar. Without a retainer, Client work would be subject to Contractor’s availability based on other assignments, would not be guaranteed, and may even become subject to a waitlist.

Streamline administrative processes to ensure that urgent Client projects do not become delayed by contracts and payments. Client will have ongoing access to Contractor without the need for an invoice and new Statement of Work for each project.

Contractor will provide proactive project management and oversight for all assigned work, and integrate closely with Client management team. While remaining an independent contractor, as defined in Section 2.1 (“Independent Contractor”) of the MSA, Contractor will offer the responsiveness, collaboration, and teamwork that Client would expect from its in-house staff.

Engagement Model

Contractor engages its clients in a retainer agreement as described in this Statement of Work. A typical engagement model consists of:

Weekly calls to discuss current ongoing projects and schedule Contractor workload for the coming week.

Monthly calls to review progress.

Additional calls as needed.

The success of the retainer will be judged by the return on investment Contractor is able to deliver to Client. Client will have monthly access to Contractor for any projects to help deliver the desired return on investment, not to exceed XX hours per month unless agreed in advance.

Contractor will proactively manage workload for Client projects on a weekly basis, primarily during weekly Client calls, to ensure steady and rapid progress. Contractor will also attempt to accommodate any last-minute requests from Client that are not planned for that week. However, requests for more than two hours of unplanned work may not be possible, or may require Contractor to reschedule an alternate project to the following week to allow room for the new assignment.

Contractor’s time will be inclusive of project management responsibilities, collaboration and check-in calls, time writing copy, and all other tasks dedicated to the progress of Client projects.

If Contractor will not be available because of travel or vacation, Contractor will coordinate with Client in advance to ensure no projects fall behind.

This Retained Services Agreement (RSA) will begin on _____________________ and continue until either party provides Notice of Termination as specified in Section 6.1 (“Notice”) of the MSA.

Should either party desire to terminate both this RSA and the MSA concurrently, the entire MSA will survive until the termination of this RSA.

Cost & Payment

Contractor incurs significant and disproportionate costs during the first 90 days of a Retained Services Agreement. They include, but are not limited to, Contractor declining other projects to ensure Client receives the Services promised under the RSA. Therefore, the terms and conditions associated with this RSA are as follows:



Contractor will charge a monthly fee of ________________ US Dollars ($X,XXX).

Contractor will submit invoices on the 15th day of each month for Services to be performed the following month. Payment is due by the last day of the month in which the invoice was sent, otherwise Contractor will cease Services beginning on the first day of the following month until Payment is made for that month.

Upon signing of the Agreement, Client guarantees to pay Contractor an upfront nonrefundable payment equal to the first three (3) monthly payments of the project (the “Guaranteed Payment”). The Guaranteed Payment is a nonrefundable payment irrespective if the Agreement is terminated by Client prior to completion of the first three (3) months. If Agreement is terminated by Contractor prior to completion of the first three (3) months, Contractor will refund to the Client an amount equal to the pro rata portion of the Guaranteed Payment. 

Client will pay the Guaranteed Payment of ________________ US Dollars ($X,XXX) at the commencement of this RSA. This payment will cover the first two months and the last month of Services. Contractor will invoice Client on a monthly basis beginning the 15th of ________________.

Should either party provide Notice of Termination for this RSA as specified in 6.1 (“Notice”) of the MSA, Contractor will continue to provide Services for the month in which Notice of Termination was given and the entire following month to allow for a smooth transition, and Client will pay for Services in this final month. For clarity, if Client issues notice of termination on January 10, Contractor will provide Services for the remainder of January and all of February, and Client will pay for these Services.

This final month’s payment is mandatory and non-refundable, even if Client would prefer to terminate this RSA immediately and does not desire or require Services from Contractor. Client will pay for the final month’s Services under the Guaranteed Payment, as defined earlier, and Contractor will not issue a refund for the final month’s payment.

All other payment terms, except those explicitly outline above, are defined in Section 5 (“Fees, Invoices, Payment”) of the MSA.

Ownership

As long as Client completes payments according to the timeline above, for the purposes of Section 2.2 (“Ownership”) of the MSA, full payment for each Final Deliverable will be considered made immediately upon Client approval and/or publication.

Should Client miss a payment according to the schedule above, beginning on the date the payment was due Contractor will retain ownership as defined under Section 2.2 (“Ownership”) of any Final Deliverables delivered to Client during that timeframe. Ownership pursuant to Section 2.2 (“Ownership”) will not be transferred to Client for such Deliverables until the missed payment(s) is furnished to Contractor.  For the avoidance of doubt, Client will retain ownership of any Final Deliverable that was approved and/or published prior to the date of the missed payment. Client may not approve or publish any new Final Deliverables until the missed payment is made.

Expiration

This Statement of Work is null and void if not executed within fourteen (14) days.
